name: Home Assistant
short_description: Open-source, local-first home automation platform that unifies thousands of smart devices and runs a private on-device voice assistant.
long_description: Home Assistant is a free, open-source platform started by Paulus Schoutsen in 2013 and now stewarded by the non-profit Open Home Foundation. It integrates with over a thousand brands of sensors, appliances, and robots, runs automations and dashboards locally rather than in the cloud, and includes a built-in voice assistant called Assist. As a control layer that senses and acts across an entire household, it shows how physical AI in daily life can be assembled from distributed everyday objects while keeping data private and under the user's control.

images.caption: Home Assistant Green, Yellow, and Blue home automation hubs that run the platform locally.
images.alt_text: Three small Home Assistant single-board computer hubs in green, yellow, and blue cases sitting side by side on a desk.

sources.title: Home Assistant
sources.access_notes: Official project homepage describing local control, integrations, and the Assist voice assistant.

sources.title: Home Assistant
sources.access_notes: Founding year (2013, Paulus Schoutsen), Apache 2.0 license, Open Home Foundation governance, Nabu Casa cloud service.

interactions.title: Talk to the house without the cloud
interactions.description: Say "Hey Jarvis, turn off the kitchen" to a dedicated voice puck and have speech-to-text, intent, and the reply all run on the box in the closet — no audio ever leaves the home.

interactions.title: Give the house a personality
interactions.description: Wire an LLM in as the conversation agent and hand it a character prompt, so asking about the weather comes back answered in the voice of Super Mario instead of a flat status read-out.
interactions.source_label: Home Assistant — create an AI personality

interactions.title: Plain-language commands an LLM acts on
interactions.description: Expose your entities to an OpenAI or local Ollama agent and say "it's getting dark in here" — the model maps the request to the actual lights it's allowed to touch and turns them on.

interactions.title: A nudge when the laundry is actually done
interactions.description: People put a power-monitoring smart plug on the washing machine and watch the wattage: when draw drops below a threshold for a few minutes the cycle is over, and the phone buzzes — sometimes nagging until the door is opened.

interactions.title: "Wasp in a box" so the lights don't quit on you
interactions.description: A community pattern treats a person like a wasp trapped in a room: once motion is seen and the door stays shut, occupancy holds even while you sit perfectly still reading, so the lights don't snap off mid-chapter.
interactions.source_label: HA community — Wasp in the Box blueprint

interactions.title: Pretend someone's home while you're away
interactions.description: Owners replay their own logged routines on vacation — lights, blinds, and the TV switching at slightly randomized times — so an empty house looks lived-in to anyone casing it from the street.

interactions.title: A house that whispers instead of barking
interactions.description: Tie the existing motion sensors and time-of-day together so that after midnight the hallway light wakes only to a dim warm glow and any spoken reply drops to a near-whisper — grounded in the motion, time, and local TTS the platform already runs.
